title: L2 Support Engineer
description:
Summary:

The Level 2 Engineer will provide operational and application support for mission-critical 24x7 systems. This role involves incident and problem management, change and patch management, documentation, configuration management, testing, and knowledge management. The engineer will ensure system availability, perform root cause analysis, and maintain operational documentation.

Requirements:

  • Provide Level 2 support operations for mission-critical systems and infrastructure.
  • Troubleshoot and diagnose incidents escalated from Level 1 support.
  • Ensure adherence to SLAs and system availability.
  • Investigate and resolve application incidents, perform root cause analysis, and document resolutions.
  • Monitor application logs and integration points such as REST APIs and message queues.
  • Perform application health checks and liaise with Level 3 support for complex issues.
  • Manage P1/P2 incidents within SLA, perform post-incident reviews, and recommend fixes.
  • Conduct operational impact assessments and present changes to the Change Advisory Board.
  • Perform patch management, including readiness, stakeholder coordination, and post-patch validation.
  • Maintain operational and configuration documentation, including SOPs, incident response checklists, and knowledge base articles.
  • Ensure environment consistency across DEV, IVVQ, ISO-PROD, UAT, and PROD.
  • Conduct operational readiness testing and post-change verification.
  • Document resolutions and contribute to the knowledge base.
  • Gather logs and system metrics for root cause analysis and use diagnostic tools to analyse application behaviour.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
  • At least 5 years of experience in Level 2 support for mission-critical 24x7 production environments, preferably in the public sector.
  • At least 2 years in a team lead or supervisory role, coordinating tasks and mentoring junior engineers.
  • Proven experience handling P1/P2 incidents and managing post-incident reviews.
  • Certification in Red Hat Enterprise Linux or Kubernetes is a plus.
  • Strong knowledge of RHEL and Windows Server, networking fundamentals, middleware, and infrastructure (Nginx, Kubernetes, Docker, Spring Boot).
  • Experience with message queues (IBM MQ, Kafka), programming languages (Java, C#, Golang), and databases (SQL Server, PostgreSQL).
  • Familiarity with ITIL/ITSM processes, security awareness, and DR/HA concepts.
  • Strong technical, leadership, communication, and collaboration skills.
  • Ability to maintain composure under pressure, attention to detail, and a continuous learning mindset.
  • Eligibility to obtain G50 security clearance;

postedCompany.description:
Newtone Consulting is an IT recruitment and sourcing specialist, delivering tailored talent solutions since 2013. We partner with organisations to identify, select, and onboard the right IT professionals.

With deep expertise across the IT landscape, we recruit for a wide range of roles, from functional business analysts to highly specialised technical architects. Our consultants bring strong IT backgrounds and years of recruitment experience, enabling them to understand complex technical requirements and match them with the right talent efficiently and accurately.

We work with leading multinational clients across banking, technology, fintech, industrial, infrastructure, and retail sectors, supporting their growth and critical digital initiatives through high-quality IT talent.
